title: FIFA Congress Vancouver Now 4 Days Away — Iran Fate, 2031 Women's World Cup Bid on Agenda
detail: With the 76th FIFA Congress at Vancouver Convention Centre now just four days away (April 30), delegates from all 211 FIFA member nations are traveling to Canada. The Congress is the formal decision point for Iran's World Cup participation crisis: Secretary of State Rubio's April 24 statement cleared Iranian athletes but barred IRGC-affiliated personnel, and Congress will determine next steps for the 47-day countdown. A second major agenda item is the formal vote on the 2031 FIFA Women's World Cup host — an uncontested joint bid by the USA, Mexico, Costa Rica, and Jamaica, launched in October 2025 and backed by US Soccer Federation; approval would mark the first Women's World Cup in North America since 1999. Palestine Football Association president Jibril Rajoub and two other Palestinian football officials remain unable to attend after being denied Canadian visas on April 14, preventing Palestinian leadership from formally presenting their case to FIFA's ruling on Israeli settlement clubs — a decision that drew sharp criticism from human rights organizations. BC Place stadium in Vancouver — one of Canada's two World Cup host venues — is serving as a focal point for pre-tournament media and fan engagement events in the leadup to the Congress.

media.caption: Vancouver Convention Centre hosts the 76th FIFA Congress on April 30 — Iran's World Cup fate and the 2031 Women's World Cup host vote headline the agenda, with delegates from 211 nations converging on Canada

title: World Cup Final Tickets Reach $2.3 Million on Resale — Cheapest Standard Final Seat $10,990
detail: CNN reported that four Category 1 seats for the July 19 World Cup Final at MetLife Stadium (East Rutherford, NJ) were listed on secondary markets at $2,299,998.85 each — making them among the most expensive tickets in the history of professional sport. The cheapest available standard Final ticket on the secondary market is $10,990 (the FIFA face-value Cat 1 price), while wheelchair-accessible seats fetch nearly $15,000. FIFA's own official resale platform — which had previously listed seats at $143,750 — now features listings that have exceeded $2 million. An Al Jazeera investigation on April 23 also revealed that ticket demand has not been as uniform as FIFA suggested: some matches in smaller markets show lingering supply, while the April 22 inventory drop for 'last-minute' sales replenished tickets in the $60 Supporter Entry Tier for fans who missed prior windows. Football Supporters Europe (FSE) and Euroconsumers' European Commission anti-trust complaint filed April 3 remains under assessment. NJ Transit's $150 round-trip fare from Penn Station to MetLife — confirmed with zero FIFA contribution to transit costs — continues to generate political backlash from New Jersey Governor Sherill and US Congress members. FIFA President Infantino has defended pricing as reflecting genuine demand at 'a very special market.'

media.caption: World Cup Final tickets listed at $2.3 million per seat on secondary markets — the cheapest available standard Final seat is $10,990, as the ticket pricing controversy deepens ahead of the July 19 MetLife Stadium Final

title: Mohamed Salah Out for Liverpool Season — Egypt Official Confirms Hamstring Injury Ahead of World Cup
detail: Mohamed Salah suffered a hamstring injury during Liverpool's match against Crystal Palace on April 26 and an Egypt team official confirmed the same day that his club season with Liverpool is effectively over. The Egyptian national team director stated Salah had sustained the injury and would not return for Liverpool's remaining Premier League fixtures. Egypt's World Cup medical staff projected a four-week recovery window, which — if the timeline holds — would see Salah fit for Egypt's Group H opener on June 15 vs. Belgium at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia. Salah, 33, is Egypt's captain and the tournament's most high-profile African player, having been the continent's top scorer in the 2025–26 Champions League group stage. His absence for the rest of the club season is significant for Liverpool but the World Cup timeline appears manageable barring complications. Egypt also face France (June 20, Lumen Field, Seattle) and Iraq (June 25, Gillette Stadium, Boston) in Group H. The injury adds Salah to a growing list of major stars navigating fitness concerns as the June 11 tournament opening approaches — alongside Lamine Yamal (Spain, hamstring), Xavi Simons (Netherlands, ACL), Rodrygo (Brazil, ACL/meniscus), and Hugo Ekitike (France, Achilles).

media.caption: Mohamed Salah's Liverpool season ends with hamstring injury vs Crystal Palace — Egypt confirm captain's club season is over but project World Cup return fit for the June 15 opener vs Belgium
